behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser/range-resolution/function-evaluator handoff, not just SUM arithmetic. A pure unit test of SUM can miss bugs in expanding `A1:A10` into cell values. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is only in-memory evaluation (`=SUM(A1:A10)`), with no OS/network/subprocess and no AI output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Build an in-memory workbook with A1:A10 populated, run full formula evaluation for a target cell with `=SUM(A1:A10)`, and assert computed value matches expected sum.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module dependency chaining (reference resolution + expression evaluation + propagation). The real failure appears at module handoff/state wiring across cells. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is deterministic local formula chaining with no real OS/network/subprocess and no model call.
behaviors.test_description: Create in-memory cells `A1=3`, `B1==A1*2`, `C1==B1+1`, evaluate C1 through the engine, and assert C1==7 with B1 intermediate consistent.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection depends on dependency graph construction plus evaluator traversal behavior. A single-function unit test can miss integration bugs where graph metadata is wired incorrectly. Rejected higher levels because behavior is in-memory graph logic only; no real infra or AI dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Construct workbook formulas `A1==B1+1` and `B1==A1+1`, trigger evaluation, and assert a circular-reference error is returned quickly (no infinite recursion/loop).

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets function semantics (exact-match lookup + not-found error) and can fail entirely within local VLOOKUP logic. No required module boundary is stated beyond function execution. Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require cross-module state handoff; rejected higher levels because no OS/network/subprocess or AI.
behaviors.test_description: Call VLOOKUP function directly with deterministic table inputs: one matching key and one missing key; assert returned value for match and specific error for miss.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`).
behaviors.justification: Failure is in local conditional-expression semantics for nested IF branching. This can be caught at one function/object boundary with deterministic inputs. Rejected Integration/System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text specifies logic evaluation only, no external infra or model output.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate nested IF expression with representative A1 values (e.g., 5, 15, 25) in a local evaluator unit and assert outputs `low`, `medium`, `high` respectively.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is string-join rule correctness (separator placement, empty-value handling) inside CONCATENATE logic. No mandatory module interaction is needed to surface this failure. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is deterministic string function behavior without real infra or AI.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke CONCATENATE logic with values including empties and a separator; assert empties are skipped and separators appear only between non-empty tokens.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-sheet name resolution + workbook/sheet lookup + evaluator handoff. Unit-testing arithmetic alone cannot catch sheet-resolution wiring bugs. Rejected System because behavior only states reference resolution in workbook memory, not disk/network/OS behavior; rejected Agentic/Workflow due no model steps.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with `Sheet2!A1=10` and formula `Sheet1!B1==Sheet2!A1+1`; evaluate `Sheet1!B1` and assert 11 with successful sheet reference resolution.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: This is dependency-graph invalidation + recalculation orchestration across modules. Real failure appears in update propagation order/state wiring, not a single local function. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Set up dependency chain/diamond in-memory, evaluate once, mutate an upstream cell, trigger recompute, and assert all downstream cells match expected values consistent with topological recalculation.

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Undo correctness spans history stack, formula storage, and dependency recomputation state. A unit test of undo stack alone can miss restored-value propagation failures. Rejected System despite persistence feature elsewhere because this behavior text is about in-session undo after edit, not disk I/O; rejected Agentic/Workflow due no AI.
behaviors.test_description: In-memory workbook: compute dependents, edit an upstream formula causing changed results, execute undo, then assert original formula text and all dependent computed values are restored.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real disk persistence (`to disk`/`loading back`) and binary serialization fidelity. Real filesystem I/O and actual serializer-deserializer contract are part of the failure boundary; mocks can hide corruption/precision issues. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness is deterministic serialization, not AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Write a workbook with formulas and sensitive floating-point values to a real temp file using production save path, reload via production load path, and assert exact formula text and value equivalence (with strict precision expectations).
